mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from an already low level, discount rates and refinancing pressure decline faster than earnings expectations adjust, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. The signal fails if rate cuts are already fully priced or if cuts coincide with acute recession stress that overwhelms the discount-rate effect.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% indicates near-zero policy stance
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS instances do not produce positive 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a local threshold signals weakening income growth and higher recession probability, which should depress equity prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is rising rather than merely flat.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.6% is elevated versus neutral labor-market conditions
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, it indicates broad-based final demand and payroll income growth that support near-term earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The joint condition is necessary because sales strength without payroll gains is less informative about sustainable demand.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and PAYEMS are both available with recent monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if the two-variable interaction does not outperform each proxy alone, or if joint-expansion episodes fail to deliver positive 20d SPX returns more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained improvement in INDPRO implies rising factory output and better operating leverage for cyclicals, which should lift equities over a quarter as earnings breadth expands. The signal fails when industrial production is stagnant or falling, because then supply-side momentum is absent.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is 103.5966, indicating ongoing industrial activity
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum is not followed by positive 60d SPX returns in a majority of instances, or if the predictive relationship vanishes in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above trend while FEDFUNDS remains near zero creates a policy-backdrop mismatch that raises real-rate and taper-risk concerns, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if inflation rises without any associated policy repricing or if equities remain insensitive to the inflation surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes under low policy rates do not produce negative 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant and stable across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals softer housing demand, weaker construction income, and lower spillovers to consumer spending, so S&P 500 returns should weaken over the next quarter. The effect should be clearer when housing starts are falling across multiple months rather than in a single volatile print.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 550.0 is available as a timely monthly housing indicator
failure_condition: Reject if three-month housing declines are not followed by below-zero 60d SPX returns on average, or if the signal is indistinguishable from noise after revision-adjusted testing.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE rises, labor income and job security deteriorate simultaneously, creating a stronger equity headwind than either series alone. This joint deterioration should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE condition fails to improve hit rate versus each series separately, or if triggered episodes do not deliver negative 20d SPX returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDP growth impulse with stable retail sales indicates broad nominal demand that supports earnings revisions and higher equity prices over the following quarter. The signal is not valid when GDP is slowing or retail sales weaken concurrently, because then aggregate demand is not expanding.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 accelerates versus prior quarter and RSAFS is flat-to-rising over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if GDP-plus-sales expansions do not outperform a simple GDP-only or sales-only signal, or if positive 60d SPX returns do not dominate in triggered observations.
